title: Accountant Job at Hana-Mera Recruitment
content:
(adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    

At Hana-Mera Recruitment, we specialize in helping startups hire smart, not just fast. We understand the unique pressure founders face: tight timelines, limited resources, and the critical need to make every hire count. One wrong hire can slow momentum. The right one? Game-changing. With a proven track record of helping startups across Nigeria, India, the US, and beyond, we’ve successfully placed high-impact talent across product, engineering, operations, growth, and leadership. From seed stage founders making their first hire to scaling startups expanding globally, we’ve been the trusted partner behind growth. But we’re not just here for startups, we’re here for talent, too. We help job seekers become standout candidates through CV revamps, LinkedIn optimization, and personalized career strategy. Whether you’re breaking into the startup world or taking the next big step, we help you get seen and get hired. We know startups. We know talent. And we know how to bring the right people together when it matters most.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant
Job Location: Surulere, Lagos
Work Type: Hybrid (Once in a week onsite)

Job Summary


	We’re looking for a detail-oriented Accountant to manage records, prepare reports, and support business growth.


Responsibilities


	Track daily transactions
	Prepare weekly/monthly reports
	Monitor cash flow & expenses
	Reconcile bank statements


Requirements


	1–2 years of accounting experience
	Knowledge of accounting principles
	Proficiency in Excel/software
	Detail-oriented & reliable
	Professional certification is an added advantage


Work Structure:


	4 physical visits monthly
	Remote support as needed.


Salary
N150,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of the    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Junior Account Officer",
  "description": "TheHRHive -&nbsp;Our client, Feratto Industries Limited is the largest manufacturer and supplier of high-quality polystyrene/styrofoam products in Nigeria. With a robust installed production capacity, we are capable of meeting any quantity of demand at any given time. We are dedicated to operational excellence and delivering top-tier products to our clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Junior Account Officer
Job Location:&nbsp;&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and motivated Junior Account Officer to join our finance team.
	The successful candidate will provide crucial support to the accounting department by assisting in the management of financial records, processing transactions, and ensuring the accuracy and completeness of financial data.
	This is an excellent opportunity for an early-career professional looking to build a solid foundation in accounting within a dynamic manufacturing environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in maintaining accurate and up-to-date financial records, including accounts payable and receivable ledgers.
	Process invoices, payments, and receipts in a timely manner.
	Perform regular bank reconciliations to ensure accuracy between company records and bank statements.
	Support the month-end and year-end closing processes.
	Assist in the preparation of financial reports, spreadsheets, and statements as directed by senior accountants.
	Help monitor and manage company expenses and petty cash.
	Provide administrative support to the finance department, including filing, data entry, and correspondence.
	Liaise with vendors and clients regarding billing and payment inquiries.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and relevant accounting standards.
	Undertake any other ad-hoc tasks or projects as assigned by the line manager or senior finance staff.


Qualifications and Skills


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
	1-3 years of relevant experience in an accounting or finance role (internship experience will be considered).
	Basic understanding of accounting principles and practices.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel.
	Experience with accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks, Sage) is an added advantage.
	Strong numerical and analytical skills with a high level of attention to detail.
	Good organizational and time management skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks.
	Effective communication and interpersonal skills.
	Candidates with immediate availablity will be given priority
	Female candidates preferred
	A proactive and positive attitude, with a willingness to learn and grow within the company.


Salary
N200,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resumes and portfolios to:&nbsp;thehrhive@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Junior Account Officer Job at HR Hive




        
    

        
        TheHRHive - Our client, Feratto Industries Limited is the largest manufacturer and supplier of high-quality polystyrene/styrofoam products in Nigeria. With a robust installed production capacity, we are capable of meeting any quantity of demand at any given time. We are dedicated to operational excellence and delivering top-tier products to our clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Junior Account Officer
Job Location:  Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a detail-oriented and motivated Junior Account Officer to join our finance team.
	The successful candidate will provide crucial support to the accounting department by assisting in the management of financial records, processing transactions, and ensuring the accuracy and completeness of financial data.
	This is an excellent opportunity for an early-career professional looking to build a solid foundation in accounting within a dynamic manufacturing environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in maintaining accurate and up-to-date financial records, including accounts payable and receivable ledgers.
	Process invoices, payments, and receipts in a timely manner.
	Perform regular bank reconciliations to ensure accuracy between company records and bank statements.
	Support the month-end and year-end closing processes.
	Assist in the preparation of financial reports, spreadsheets, and statements as directed by senior accountants.
	Help monitor and manage company expenses and petty cash.
	Provide administrative support to the finance department, including filing, data entry, and correspondence.
	Liaise with vendors and clients regarding billing and payment inquiries.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and relevant accounting standards.
	Undertake any other ad-hoc tasks or projects as assigned by the line manager or senior finance staff.


Qualifications and Skills


	Bachelor's Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
	1-3 years of relevant experience in an accounting or finance role (internship experience will be considered).
	Basic understanding of accounting principles and practices.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel.
	Experience with accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks, Sage) is an added advantage.
	Strong numerical and analytical skills with a high level of attention to detail.
	Good organizational and time management skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks.
	Effective communication and interpersonal skills.
	Candidates with immediate availablity will be given priority
	Female candidates preferred
	A proactive and positive attitude, with a willingness to learn and grow within the company.


Salary
N200,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resumes and portfolios to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Multi Unit Restaurant Manager",
  "description": "Uncle Stans Food is a quick service restaurant (QSR),a fast-food model built for speed and convenience, featuring a limited, standardized menu of items like small shops or pizza. Operating primarily through counter-service and drive-thrus, it focuses on rapid food assembly from pre-prepared components, ensuring consistent, affordable meals delivered in minutes. Our functional, casual environment prioritizes efficient turnover over prolonged dining, making it a dominant global industry by fulfilling the demand for reliable, on-the-go satisfaction.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Multi Unit Restaurant Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Role Summary:


	The Multi Unit Restaurant Manager is responsible for&nbsp;overseeing all outlet operations, ensuring&nbsp;smooth daily running, consistent service quality, operational efficiency, and achievement of sales and performance targets across all Uncle Stan&rsquo;s Foods locations. This role is execution-heavy and results-driven.


Key Responsibilities
Outlet Operations Oversight:


	Supervise day-to-day operations of all outlets to ensure uninterrupted service.
	Ensure outlets open and close on time, fully staffed and operational.
	Conduct regular outlet inspections to enforce SOPs and brand standards.
	Resolve operational issues promptly to avoid service disruption or revenue loss.


Performance &amp; KPI Management:


	Set, monitor, and enforce KPIs for outlet managers and supervisors.
	Track daily sales, wastage, service speed, customer complaints, and stock usage.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly operational performance reports for MD.


Staff Supervision &amp; Discipline:


	Supervise outlet managers and ensure proper staff deployment.
	Enforce discipline, attendance, grooming, and conduct standards.
	Recommend rewards, sanctions, or terminations based on performance.
	Identify training gaps and coordinate operational training with HR.


Stock &amp; Supply Chain Coordination:


	Work closely with Supply Chain to ensure product availability across outlets.
	Prevent stockouts, pilferage, and excessive wastage.
	Validate daily stock reports and escalate discrepancies immediately.


Quality Control &amp; Customer Experience:


	Ensure food quality, portion control, hygiene, and presentation are consistent.
	Monitor customer feedback and complaints; implement corrective actions.
	Ensure compliance with food safety and health regulations.


Cost Control &amp; Efficiency;


	Monitor operational costs and identify cost-saving opportunities.
	Reduce wastage, inefficiencies, and downtime across outlets.
	Ensure efficient use of manpower, utilities, and resources.


Process Improvement &amp; Expansion Support


	Improve operational processes for speed, quality, and scalability.
	Support new outlet openings, launches, and operational setups.
	Ensure new outlets meet Uncle Stan&rsquo;s Foods operational standards from day one.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Business, Operations Management, Hospitality, or related field.
	Minimum&nbsp;5&ndash;8 years of experience&nbsp;in QSR, retail, or multi-outlet food operations.
	Proven experience managing&nbsp;multiple outlets simultaneously.
	Strong leadership, decision-making, and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent reporting, communication, and organizational skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and handle crisis situations.
	Strong understanding of SOPs, food operations, and people management.


Key Competencies:


	Operational excellence
	Strong leadership &amp; accountability
	Attention to detail
	Data-driven decision-making
	High integrity &amp; discipline
	Customer-focused mindset.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hrunclestansfoods@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Multi Unit Restaurant Manager Job at Uncle Stan's Food




        
    

        
        Uncle Stans Food is a quick service restaurant (QSR),a fast-food model built for speed and convenience, featuring a limited, standardized menu of items like small shops or pizza. Operating primarily through counter-service and drive-thrus, it focuses on rapid food assembly from pre-prepared components, ensuring consistent, affordable meals delivered in minutes. Our functional, casual environment prioritizes efficient turnover over prolonged dining, making it a dominant global industry by fulfilling the demand for reliable, on-the-go satisfaction.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Multi Unit Restaurant Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Role Summary:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Multi Unit Restaurant Manager is responsible for overseeing all outlet operations, ensuring smooth daily running, consistent service quality, operational efficiency, and achievement of sales and performance targets across all Uncle Stan’s Foods locations. This role is execution-heavy and results-driven.


Key Responsibilities
Outlet Operations Oversight:


	Supervise day-to-day operations of all outlets to ensure uninterrupted service.
	Ensure outlets open and close on time, fully staffed and operational.
	Conduct regular outlet inspections to enforce SOPs and brand standards.
	Resolve operational issues promptly to avoid service disruption or revenue loss.


Performance & KPI Management:


	Set, monitor, and enforce KPIs for outlet managers and supervisors.
	Track daily sales, wastage, service speed, customer complaints, and stock usage.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly operational performance reports for MD.


Staff Supervision & Discipline:


	Supervise outlet managers and ensure proper staff deployment.
	Enforce discipline, attendance, grooming, and conduct standards.
	Recommend rewards, sanctions, or terminations based on performance.
	Identify training gaps and coordinate operational training with HR.


Stock & Supply Chain Coordination:


	Work closely with Supply Chain to ensure product availability across outlets.
	Prevent stockouts, pilferage, and excessive wastage.
	Validate daily stock reports and escalate discrepancies immediately.


Quality Control & Customer Experience:


	Ensure food quality, portion control, hygiene, and presentation are consistent.
	Monitor customer feedback and complaints; implement corrective actions.
	Ensure compliance with food safety and health regulations.


Cost Control & Efficiency;


	Monitor operational costs and identify cost-saving opportunities.
	Reduce wastage, inefficiencies, and downtime across outlets.
	Ensure efficient use of manpower, utilities, and resources.


Process Improvement & Expansion Support


	Improve operational processes for speed, quality, and scalability.
	Support new outlet openings, launches, and operational setups.
	Ensure new outlets meet Uncle Stan’s Foods operational standards from day one.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in Business, Operations Management, Hospitality, or related field.
	Minimum 5–8 years of experience in QSR, retail, or multi-outlet food operations.
	Proven experience managing multiple outlets simultaneously.
	Strong leadership, decision-making, and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent reporting, communication, and organizational skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and handle crisis situations.
	Strong understanding of SOPs, food operations, and people management.


Key Competencies:


	Operational excellence
	Strong leadership & accountability
	Attention to detail
	Data-driven decision-making
	High integrity & discipline
	Customer-focused mindset.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Safety Officer",
  "description": "

Primovet Engineering Limited is a proactive engineering construction company built on a culture of excellence and innovation.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:


Job Position: Safety Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time



Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive Safety Officer to ensure compliance with health and safety regulations across our project sites and office operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Enforce HSE policies and safety procedures
	Conduct site inspections and risk assessments
	Monitor use of PPE and safe work practices
	Investigate incidents and prepare reports
	Deliver safety briefings/toolbox talks


Requirements


	HSE certification (e.g., HSE 1, 2 &amp; 3)
	2&ndash;4 years of experience (construction preferred)
	Knowledge of safety regulations and compliance
	Strong attention to detail and reporting skills




Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@primovetengineering.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail

Safety Officer Job at Primovet Engineering Limited




        
    

        
        

Primovet Engineering Limited is a proactive engineering construction company built on a culture of excellence and innovation.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:


Job Position: Safety Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time



Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a proactive Safety Officer to ensure compliance with health and safety regulations across our project sites and office operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Enforce HSE policies and safety procedures
	Conduct site inspections and risk assessments
	Monitor use of PPE and safe work practices
	Investigate incidents and prepare reports
	Deliver safety briefings/toolbox talks


Requirements


	HSE certification (e.g., HSE 1, 2 & 3)
	2–4 years of experience (construction preferred)
	Knowledge of safety regulations and compliance
	Strong attention to detail and reporting skills




Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail

    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Store Manager Job at Hana-Mera Recruitment
content:
(adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    

At Hana-Mera Recruitment, we specialize in helping startups hire smart, not just fast. We understand the unique pressure founders face: tight timelines, limited resources, and the critical need to make every hire count. One wrong hire can slow momentum. The right one? Game-changing. With a proven track record of helping startups across Nigeria, India, the US, and beyond, we’ve successfully placed high-impact talent across product, engineering, operations, growth, and leadership. From seed stage founders making their first hire to scaling startups expanding globally, we’ve been the trusted partner behind growth. But we’re not just here for startups, we’re here for talent, too. We help job seekers become standout candidates through CV revamps, LinkedIn optimization, and personalized career strategy. Whether you’re breaking into the startup world or taking the next big step, we help you get seen and get hired. We know startups. We know talent. And we know how to bring the right people together when it matters most.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Store Manager

Job Location: Wuse 2, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for a reliable, detail-oriented, and results-driven Store Manager to oversee the daily operations of our Abuja store.
	The ideal candidate is proactive, organized, and customer-focused, with the ability to manage people, drive sales, and create a welcoming in-store experience.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee day-to-day store operations to ensure efficiency and productivity
	Prepare the store for daily opening and ensure smooth closing processes
	Track and record daily sales, expenses, and transactions accurately
	Maintain a clean, organized, and visually appealing store environment
	Supervise store assistants and coordinate effectively with delivery riders
	Provide regular performance updates and reports
	Implement creative strategies to increase walk-in customers and sales


Requirements


	Proven experience in retail, sales, or store management
	Strong communication and customer relationship skills
	Good organizational and multitasking abilities
	Ability to work independently and take initiative
	High level of integrity, responsibility, and accountability
	Must be based in Abuja (preferably around Wuse 2)


What We’re Looking For:


	A smart, professional, and approachable personality
	Strong attention to detail and consistency in execution
	A goal-oriented mindset with a passion for delivering results
	Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment
	Basic knowledge of social media or online sales is an added advantage


Work Environment & Culture:


	Supportive and respectful team environment
	Performance-based recognition and growth opportunities
	Structured but flexible approach to getting work done


Diversity & Inclusion


	We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to building a diverse team.


Salary
N150,000/month + Performance Bonus.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of the email.

Note


	We strongly encourage qualified female candidates to apply.
	Only shortlisted candidates will be    

    

        Click here to Apply
